A nested loop checking each dictionary root against each word works, but it takes O(M * N) time, which might be slow if the sentence is very long.
Instead, build a Trie out of the `dictionary` roots. This takes O(Total length of all roots) time and groups common prefixes together.

Split the `sentence` into individual words. For each word, traverse the Trie letter by letter. Stop as soon as you reach a Trie node marked as 'end of a word' and replace the original word with the discovered string path. If you hit a dead-end before finding a root node, the word isn't replaceable.
